intro: Megistostigma glabratum is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, commonly known as Indian snake root, and belongs to the family Zingiberaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Megistostigma glabratum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
